data.content:
The file is generated but not added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** (before any targets run), so the file created during the build is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so Roslyn sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk, but MSBuild's glob-based file inclusion happens at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up by the implicit `<Compile Include="**/*.cs" />` glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it in `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since this is exactly the scenario it covers.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so Roslyn sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild's implicit `<Compile Include="**/*.cs" />` glob expands at **evaluation time**, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` items inside the target so the generated file is explicitly included in compilation and properly cleaned up.
